name: Edge Cases and Polish
theme: Boundary conditions, malformed input, error recovery, and run.sh integration verification
features: F8: Edge Case and Error Handling Tests (meta-tests/test-edge-cases.bats)

criteria.description: meta-tests/test-edge-cases.bats exists, is a valid bats file, and loads the test helper via 'load helpers/test-helper'
testSteps: Verify meta-tests/test-edge-cases.bats exists and is non-empty
Verify it contains a bats shebang or is loadable by bats
Verify it contains "load 'helpers/test-helper'" or equivalent

criteria.description: All tests in test-edge-cases.bats pass when run via 'bats meta-tests/test-edge-cases.bats'
testSteps: Run: bats meta-tests/test-edge-cases.bats
Verify exit code is 0
Verify no tests show as 'not ok'

criteria.description: The full meta-tests suite still passes (no regressions): 'bash meta-tests/run.sh' exits 0
testSteps: Run: bash meta-tests/run.sh
Verify exit code is 0
Verify summary line appears showing completion time

criteria.description: test-edge-cases.bats contains at least 15 @test blocks covering edge cases across multiple modules
testSteps: Count lines matching '@test ' in test-edge-cases.bats
Verify count >= 15

criteria.description: Test: slugify with a string containing only special characters (e.g., '!@#$%^&*()') returns empty string
testSteps: Verify a @test exists that passes a string of only special characters to slugify
Verify the test asserts the output is an empty string

criteria.description: Test: slugify with a string containing only spaces returns empty string
testSteps: Verify a @test exists that passes a string of only spaces (e.g., '     ') to slugify
Verify the test asserts the output is an empty string

criteria.description: Test: json_read with a binary file does not crash and returns empty string
testSteps: Verify a @test creates a binary file (e.g., printf '\x00\x01\x02' > file) and calls json_read on it
Verify the test asserts exit code 0 (graceful handling) and output is empty

criteria.description: Test: json_read with truncated JSON (e.g., '{"name": ') returns empty string without crashing
testSteps: Verify a @test creates a file containing truncated/incomplete JSON
Verify the test calls json_read and asserts graceful handling (exit 0, empty output)

criteria.description: Test: json_read with a deeply nested path (e.g., '.a.b.c.d.e') on a file that has that nested structure returns the correct value
testSteps: Verify a @test creates a deeply nested JSON file and calls json_read with a multi-level path
Verify the test asserts the correct deeply nested value is returned

criteria.description: Test: init_harness_state with a prompt containing double quotes, single quotes, and backslashes produces valid JSON in config.json
testSteps: Verify a @test calls init_harness_state with a prompt like 'Build a "great" app with \'features\' and C:\\path'
Verify the test asserts config.json is valid JSON (jq parses it without error)
Verify the test asserts userPrompt field contains the original prompt text

criteria.description: Test: init_harness_state with a prompt containing newlines produces valid JSON in config.json
testSteps: Verify a @test calls init_harness_state with a prompt containing literal newline characters
Verify the test asserts config.json is valid JSON (jq -e . returns 0)
Verify the userPrompt field preserves the newline content

criteria.description: Test: update_handoff called before init_harness_state creates handoff.json from scratch with correct structure
testSteps: Verify a @test ensures no handoff.json exists, then calls update_handoff
Verify the test asserts handoff.json is created with completedSprints array, currentSprint, and git fields

criteria.description: Test: update_regression_registry with malformed (non-parseable) contract JSON does not crash and does not corrupt the registry
testSteps: Verify a @test creates a contract.json with invalid JSON content (e.g., 'not json at all')
Verify the test calls update_regression_registry and asserts it does not crash (exit 0 or handles error)
Verify the registry.json remains unchanged/valid after the call

criteria.description: Test: git_create_sprint_branch fails gracefully when the harness branch does not exist
testSteps: Verify a @test initializes a git repo but does NOT create the harness branch
Verify the test calls git_create_sprint_branch with a non-existent harness branch
Verify the test asserts a non-zero exit code (the function should fail, not create an orphan branch)

criteria.description: Test: on-generator-stop.sh handles multiple sprint directories with mixed statuses and picks the active one correctly
testSteps: Verify a @test creates multiple sprint directories (e.g., sprint-01 with status 'pass', sprint-02 with status 'active')
Verify on-generator-stop.sh blocks (exit 2) because the active sprint is not ready-for-eval

criteria.description: Test: hooks work correctly when HARNESS_STATE points to a non-default path (hooks hardcode 'harness-state', so this test verifies that behavior or documents the limitation)
testSteps: Verify a @test documents/tests behavior when the working directory has 'harness-state' at the expected relative path
Verify the hook reads from the correct location under the working directory

criteria.description: Test: on-stop.sh handles the case where sprint-plan.json has sprints but no corresponding sprint directories exist at all
testSteps: Verify a @test installs sprint-plan-3sprint.json but creates no sprint directories
Verify the test asserts on-stop.sh exits 0 (no directories means no in-progress sprints)

criteria.description: Test: log_cost handles non-JSON output_json argument gracefully (doesn't crash, defaults to 0 tokens)
testSteps: Verify a @test calls log_cost with a non-JSON string as the third argument
Verify the function completes without error
Verify the appended entry has inputTokens and outputTokens set to 0

criteria.description: Test: update_progress works when sprint-plan.json is missing (sprint name should be empty or handled gracefully)
testSteps: Verify a @test creates progress.md but no sprint-plan.json
Verify calling update_progress doesn't crash
Verify an entry is still appended to progress.md

criteria.description: run.sh prints a summary line containing completion time and exit code after tests finish
testSteps: Run: bash meta-tests/run.sh 2>&1
Verify output contains 'Meta-tests completed in' followed by a duration and exit code

criteria.description: run.sh --filter supports filtering tests by name pattern
testSteps: Run: bash meta-tests/run.sh --filter 'slugify' 2>&1
Verify exit code is 0
Verify output contains test names matching 'slugify' but not unrelated tests like 'sprint_pad'

criteria.description: Every @test in test-edge-cases.bats works in the test temp directory -- no test modifies the real harness-state/
testSteps: Verify tests use install_fixture or create files in $TEST_TEMP_DIR/$HARNESS_STATE
Verify no test writes to the project's actual harness-state/ directory
Verify setup() is present and creates the temp directory

criteria.description: Test names follow a 'module_or_function: edge case description' convention for readability
testSteps: Inspect all @test names in test-edge-cases.bats
Verify each follows 'function_name: description' or 'module: description' pattern (e.g., 'slugify: only special characters returns empty')
criteria.threshold: All test names follow the convention

criteria.description: Edge case tests that expect errors verify the function doesn't crash (checks exit status) AND verifies the output/state is reasonable
testSteps: Inspect @test blocks that test error/edge scenarios
Verify each checks both exit status and output/state (not just one or the other)
criteria.threshold: Error tests verify both status and output

criteria.description: The full meta-tests suite (all .bats files) contains at least 80 total @test blocks across all test files
testSteps: Run: grep -c '@test ' meta-tests/*.bats | awk -F: '{sum+=$2} END{print sum}'
Verify the total count is >= 80
criteria.threshold: >= 80 total test blocks across all .bats files

outOfScope: Modifying existing test files from sprints 2-5 (test-utils-pure.bats, test-utils-state.bats, test-git.bats, test-hooks.bats)
Testing with real Claude invocations or network calls
Modifying the harness source code (utils.sh, git.sh, hooks) -- only testing their edge cases
Modifying run.sh or the test helper library beyond what's needed for edge case support
Testing git_create_pr or git_create_issue (these require gh CLI and remote repos)
